item.title: Wald - Escaping the Delta
item.description: Author: Elijah Wald Title: Escaping the Delta: Robert Johnson and the Invention of the Blues Year: 2004 Type: Book (Amistad / HarperCollins) The Robert Johnson the twentieth century learned to revere — the haunted Delta primitive channeling a suffering older than himself, the man who met the devil a...

item.title: Muddy Waters
item.description: Genres: Chicago blues, Electric blues, Delta blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1948–1958 (Chess peak); active to 1983 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify A bottleneck slide rings out through a cranked amplifier, the note bending and sustaining far past anything an acoustic guitar could hold, and a v...

item.title: Country blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: Delta blues
item.description: Blues → Country blues → Delta blues Period: c. 1920s–1940s (recorded era); roots c.

item.title: BLUES
item.description: The blues began in the Deep South around 1900 and became the structural and emotional bedrock of nearly everything that followed — jazz, R&B, rock & roll, and rock all draw on its forms.

item.title: Abbey Road Studios
item.description: Location: 3 Abbey Road, St John’s Wood, London Years of peak activity: 1931–present (as EMI Studios until 1970, Abbey Road Studios thereafter) Key engineers: Norman Smith, Geoff Emerick, Ken Townsend, Alan Parsons EMI opened Abbey Road Studios in 1931 in a converted Georgian townhouse, and for three...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: Blues → Jump blues Period: Late 1930s–mid-1950s Location: Kansas City, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago Key influences: Swing, big band, boogie woogie, blues RYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancin...

item.title: Brill Building
item.description: Pop → Brill Building Location: 1619 Broadway, Manhattan, New York City Period: c.

item.title: Pop soul
item.description: Pop | Soul → Pop soul Period: c.

item.title: About the MAP
item.description: The MAP charts popular music as networks of influence, context, and argument.
